_readme: ORDERED-SCALE ROUTING — Signals Phase 2 prerequisite. DAVID OWNS THIS FILE.
Scoped to the SIGNALS ordered/outlier path only. It does NOT change benchmark percentiles or the global engine — the engine-global explicit-direction fix is a separate, baselined change.
Purpose: give every ordered/outlier metric an EXPLICIT magnitude order so the signal mechanism never infers rank from option-array index (the sick-pay fault class).
scale_low_to_high: the metric's real option labels ordered by MAGNITUDE, low -> high. The org's ordinal = its band's index here. This is the source of truth; array order is ignored.
na: option labels excluded from the scale (don't-know / not-applicable / unorderable). 'Not offered'/'None' that mean a genuine floor (0) are placed at the LOW end of the scale, not in na.
lens: RECOMMENDATION only — David ratifies which outcome lens (attract/retain/engage/save) each carries.
anchor_risk: the 3 metrics whose array runs opposite to the rest; resolved here with an explicit direction + scale. Not yet in any firing set — David routes them to behind or ordered_outlier.
review_reroute: metrics the brief grouped as ordered but which are NOT magnitude scales — they must NOT fire as outliers until re-routed.
thresholds: PLACEHOLDERS for the mechanism build (Mechanism A) — to be tuned against the seed panel and revisited on a real customer panel.

REW_BEN_047.conditional: gate on offers income protection; percentile within the offering cohort, n>=5 on the subset

REW_BEN_048.conditional: gate on offers income protection; percentile within the offering cohort, n>=5 on the subset

scale_low_to_high: No – study undertaken in personal time

REW_INC_061.note: carries the long-standing REW_INC_061 data flag — confirm distribution before relying on it

REW_Q049530.note: a LOW mileage threshold is the generous end (per brief). Resolved; not yet in a firing set.

REW_PAY_003.note: more frequent review is better. Resolved; not yet in a firing set.

PROP_8e0b6316.note: more frequent review is better; array was non-monotonic ('No regular cycle' listed after Quarterly). Resolved; not yet in a firing set.

REW_PRO_035.reason: NOMINAL, not a magnitude scale — performance-led vs tenure-led vs combination has no high/low end. Must not fire as an ordered-outlier. David: re-route (prevalence) or drop.

REW_PAY_TIPS_EXIST_7c80c508.reason: BINARY yes/no (tips received or not) — belongs in Phase 3 prevalence, not the ordered-outlier path.
